description: The local release gate must prove replacement-candidate restart, full cycle verification, historical retention, and invalid-cycle refusal before PR CI; remote CI remains independent release evidence rather than a duplicate local suite.
Version of the repository verification composition policy.
Allowed rollout values are legacy, record-only, shadow, and enforce; changing the value requires synchronized verification evidence.
Commands are represented and spawned as argument arrays instead of evaluated shell text.
Missing, malformed, ambiguous, or stale configuration and evidence cannot satisfy a required check.
Verification evidence is not reused between independent workflow runs.
Release verification results are not cached across commits, refs, repositories, users, or workflow runs.
Grace period before a cancelled child process group is forcefully terminated; callers may override through validated policy input.
Default per-subject timeout; subject profiles may provide a validated override.
The resource-policy adapter derives the effective worker count.
Version 1 evidence remains additive during migration and is not removed by this implementation cycle.
Version 2 evidence is recorded and compared before it is allowed to become authoritative.
As-built synchronization validation uses the run-local indexed owner and may roll back to legacy through an explicit adapter override.
Configured local regression ceiling for one valid indexed synchronization-contract pass.
Full/no-cache hook execution uses the composed runner; guarded legacy and shadow adapter overrides remain available.
Composed activation is limited to full mode with no-cache semantics.
Maximum combined stdout/stderr bytes retained for one composed subject.
Fixture copies use an isolated archive stream when filesystem copy-on-write capability is not declared.
Fixture snapshots must not use hardlinks that allow source and target mutation to cross boundaries.
Dashboard bundle and browser validation use one policy-backed owner; legacy remains an explicit rollback adapter.
Main CI uses the semantic graph and exact-owner runner; legacy workflows remain recoverable through version control.
Marker required on provider-result proof receipts.
Schema version for composed CI proof receipts.
Configured workflow identity whose proof may satisfy the composed final gate.
Configured proof-producing job identity accepted by the composed final gate.
Per-command timeout for direct-argv final-gap and fallback execution.
Maximum captured output for one direct-argv final-gap or fallback command.
Centralized locator for the compatibility hook catalog.
Centralized locator for execution classifications.
Centralized locator for aggregate coverage mappings.
Centralized locator for gap commands.
Centralized locator for version 2 evidence validation.
Centralized locator for the as-built synchronization contract.
Repository-local ignored runtime parent for same-run Dashboard manifests and locks.
Repository-local ignored Vite cache outside the immutable Dashboard source and configuration inventory.
Provider-neutral exact-owner CI graph authority.
Canonical Dashboard production-build command.
Canonical non-browser Control Center validation command.
Canonical serial Control Center browser command with machine-readable output.
Canonical aggregate fallback command executed without a shell.
Canonical synchronized documents consumed by the indexed owner.
Compatibility wiring surfaces consumed by the indexed owner.
Parent-only invariant/procedural instruction and A-F authorities consumed by the standalone owner; live product registries are excluded from CI input discovery.

Fail-closed headless Next Workflow contracts, SQLite migrations, provider/model selection, protected runtime/trust/containment owners, signed release and transition evidence, persisted authorities, external launcher boundary, CI-portable non-authorizing complete provider fixtures for bootstrap, launcher integrity, injected discovery, and development selection, the live default provider probe with read-only root and private tmpfs executable binding, and honest positive-case prerequisite skips with separate guided refusal covered by the regression owner independently of the paused Control Center.
Policy-owned fixture exclusions migrated without changing current copy behavior.
Exact source/config inventory bound to a Dashboard build manifest, including the fail-closed mutable-runtime boundary helper.
Exact test/config inventory bound to a Dashboard browser receipt.

description: Dashboard output paths, package/Vite contracts, chunk inventory, limits, and forbidden build patterns.
Serial browser worker, timeout, cancellation, and bounded input/output values for one Dashboard owner.
Task-specific resource locks owned by policy rather than runner conditionals.
Compatibility checks are adapted from the existing hook catalog instead of duplicated in runtime code.
Execution classifications are adapted from the existing parallel-group policy.
Aggregate requirements remain governed by the existing coverage authority.
Gap commands remain governed by the existing gap authority.
The strict as-built documents owner includes the synchronization-contract check and may emit both receipts in one orchestrated run.
The same-run Control Center owner builds and inspects the exact Dashboard bundle before browser validation.
The same-run Control Center owner executes the existing design-system drift check in its static phase.
The same-run Control Center owner executes the existing locale regression in its static phase.
The same-run Control Center owner executes the existing settings regression in its isolated static phase.
